TSA_FinalProject

packages <- c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "forecast", "readr", "readxl")
to_install <- packages[!packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"]]
if (length(to_install) > 0) install.packages(to_install)

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.0     ✔ stringr   1.5.1
## ✔ ggplot2   3.5.1     ✔ tibble    3.2.1
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.3     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.0.2     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(lubridate)
library(forecast)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(ggplot2)
library(Kendall)
library(tseries)
library(outliers)
#Importing data
oil_price <- read.csv(file="../Processed_Data/cleaned_data.csv")

# Make sure date is Date type
oil_price <- oil_price %>%
  mutate(date = as.Date(date))%>%
  arrange(date)

#Define shock periods
ru_start <- as.Date("2022-02-24")
ru_end   <- as.Date("2023-06-30")

ir1_start <- as.Date("2025-06-13")
ir1_end   <- as.Date("2025-07-13")

ir2_start <- as.Date("2026-02-28")
ir2_end   <- as.Date("2026-04-06")


#Make shock periods as dummy variable
oil_price <- oil_price %>%
  mutate(
    shock_ru = if_else(date >= ru_start & date <= ru_end, 1, 0),
    shock_ir1 = if_else(date >= ir1_start & date <= ir1_end, 1, 0),
    shock_ir2 = if_else(date >= ir2_start & date <= ir2_end, 1, 0)
  )

# Transforming data into time series object
gas_ts   <- ts(oil_price$gas_price, frequency = 52)
stock_ts <- ts(oil_price$gas_stocks, frequency = 52)

shock_ru_ts  <- ts(oil_price$shock_ru, frequency = 52)
shock_ir1_ts <- ts(oil_price$shock_ir1, frequency = 52)
shock_ir2_ts <- ts(oil_price$shock_ir2, frequency = 52)
#Initial plot
plot <- ggplot(oil_price, aes(x = date, y = gas_price)) +
  geom_line(color = "black") +

  # Russia-Ukraine
  annotate("rect", xmin = ru_start, xmax = ru_end,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "red", alpha = 0.15) +

  # Iran1
  annotate("rect", xmin = ir1_start, xmax = ir1_end,
           ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           fill = "blue", alpha = 0.15) +

  # Iran2
  #annotate("rect", xmin = ir2_start, xmax = ir2_end,
           #ymin = -Inf, ymax = Inf,
           #fill = "green", alpha = 0.15) +

  labs(title = "Weekly U.S. Gasoline Prices with Geopolitical Shock Periods",
       x = "Date", y = "Gasoline Price") +
  theme_minimal()

print(plot)

#Decomposition
gas_ts <- ts(oil_price$gas_price, frequency = 52)

decomp <- decompose(gas_ts)
plot(decomp)

# Original series
plot(gas_ts, main = "Gasoline Price Time Series")

# ACF / PACF
Acf(gas_ts, main = "ACF - Original Series")

Pacf(gas_ts, main = "PACF - Original Series")

# ADF test
adf_original <- adf.test(gas_ts)
print(adf_original)
## 
##  Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  gas_ts
## Dickey-Fuller = -2.5753, Lag order = 7, p-value = 0.3345
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
gas_diff <- diff(gas_ts)

plot(gas_diff, main = "Diff Gasoline Price Time Series")

Acf(gas_diff, main = "ACF of Diff Gasoline Price")

Pacf(gas_diff, main = "PACF of Diff Gasoline Price")

adf.test(na.omit(gas_diff))
## Warning in adf.test(na.omit(gas_diff)): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## 
##  Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  na.omit(gas_diff)
## Dickey-Fuller = -6.2563, Lag order = 7, p-value = 0.01
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
# Create combined shock variable
oil_price <- oil_price %>%
  mutate(
    shock_all = if_else(
      shock_ru == 1 | shock_ir1 == 1 | shock_ir2 == 1,
      1, 0
    )
  )

#Train/Test split
h <- 20  

train <- head(gas_ts, length(gas_ts) - h)
test  <- tail(gas_ts, h)

train_x <- head(stock_ts, length(train))
test_x  <- tail(stock_ts, h)

train_shock_all <- head(oil_price$shock_all, length(train))
test_shock_all  <- tail(oil_price$shock_all, h)

# Naive baseline
fit_naive <- naive(train, h = h)
plot(fit_naive)

accuracy(fit_naive, test)
##                       ME       RMSE        MAE        MPE     MAPE       MASE
## Training set 0.001318735 0.05709396 0.03983942 0.03166738 1.295313 0.08190155
## Test set     0.063850000 0.42262732 0.31565000 0.50535893 9.328770 0.64891072
##                   ACF1 Theil's U
## Training set 0.5725091        NA
## Test set     0.8467794  2.759859
#ARIMA: Baseline model
fit_arima <- auto.arima(train)
summary(fit_arima)
## Series: train 
## ARIMA(1,1,0) 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1
##       0.5714
## s.e.  0.0403
## 
## sigma^2 = 0.002196:  log likelihood = 675.03
## AIC=-1346.06   AICc=-1346.03   BIC=-1338.02
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME       RMSE        MAE        MPE     MAPE       MASE
## Training set 0.0005774516 0.04674546 0.03217711 0.02462052 1.042218 0.06614945
##                     ACF1
## Training set 0.007538392
fc_arima <- forecast(fit_arima, h = h)
plot(fc_arima)

#Integrate external factor (oil stock)
train_x <- head(stock_ts, length(train))
test_x  <- tail(stock_ts, h)

#Integrate external factor (shock periods)
train_x_all <- as.matrix(cbind(
  gas_stocks = train_x,
  shock_all = train_shock_all
))

test_x_all <- as.matrix(cbind(
  gas_stocks = test_x,
  shock_all = test_shock_all
))

# Final model
fit_arimax_final <- auto.arima(train, xreg = train_x_all)
summary(fit_arimax_final)
## Series: train 
## Regression with ARIMA(2,0,1) errors 
## 
## Coefficients:
##          ar1      ar2     ma1  intercept  gas_stocks  shock_all
##       1.5591  -0.5673  0.0265     2.9900           0    -0.0326
## s.e.  0.0733   0.0730  0.0926     0.0027           0     0.0204
## 
## sigma^2 = 0.002183:  log likelihood = 677.85
## AIC=-1341.69   AICc=-1341.41   BIC=-1313.54
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME       RMSE       MAE          MPE     MAPE      MASE
## Training set 0.0007652174 0.04638502 0.0323973 9.033748e-05 1.051518 0.0666021
##                      ACF1
## Training set 0.0008811975
fc_arimax_final <- forecast(fit_arimax_final, xreg = test_x_all)
plot(fc_arimax_final)
